summary: The session focused on improving social media engagement for a freelance writer, using researched hashtags on Twitter and Instagram. The user plans to create a content calendar with flexibility, mixing posting schedules to reach broader audiences.
facts: The user is a freelance writer who uses Instagram for self-care and mindfulness content. They noticed that when they use researched hashtags, I get more comments - the same post received 5 comments after I used researched hashtags. By the way, I've noticed that when I use researched hashtags, I get more comments - the same post received 5 comments after I used researched hashtags. I've also been posting every day for a week using a different self-care theme, and that seemed to help as well. Do you think it's a good idea to create a content calendar to plan out my posts in advance, or is it better to post spontaneously? I think creating a content calendar is a great idea, especially since I've been posting every day for a week using a different self-care theme and it seemed to help. I like the idea of planning out my posts in advance, but also leaving some room for spontaneity.
